sections.subtitle: Team Form
sections.text: Benfica shows strong form with an unbeaten streak of 10 matches, often scoring first and maintaining high possession (average 65%). Qarabağ's recent form is inconsistent, with 2 wins, 2 losses, and 1 draw in their last 5 matches, struggling defensively by conceding first in most games.

sections.subtitle: Attacking and Defensive Stats
sections.text: Benfica has been effective in attack, averaging around 1.8 goals per game in their last 5 matches, with strong expected goals (e.g., 2.96 vs Tondela). Qarabağ averages 1.6 goals scored but concedes frequently, with 1.4 goals against per game recently.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: There are no recent head-to-head matches between Benfica and Qarabağ to analyze, so predictions rely solely on current form and stats.

sections.subtitle: Game Context and Venue
sections.text: Playing at Estádio da Luz, Benfica benefits from home advantage and a superior squad depth. Qarabağ, ranked lower in European standings, may struggle in this away fixture.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, Benfica leads Qarabağ with a narrow 2-1 advantage at Estádio da Luz. The game has been competitive, with both teams showing attacking intent early on. Benfica struck first through Enzo Barrenechea's header in the 6th minute, followed by a clinical right-footed finish from Vangelis Pavlidis in the 16th. Qarabağ responded with a goal from Leandro Andrade in the 30th minute, keeping the contest alive. Both sides have created significant opportunities, but the home team holds a slight edge with more control in key areas of the pitch.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance comparison
halftimeSections.text: Benfica has shown a balanced approach, maintaining a slight edge in ball possession and creating big chances without missing any. Their entries into the final third and penalty area have been more frequent, indicating a more consistent threat upfront. Qarabağ, on the other hand, has been dangerous on the counter and managed to create big chances as well, though they've missed crucial opportunities. Defensively, both teams have been evenly matched in duels and aerial challenges, but Qarabağ's higher expected goals suggest they’ve had quality chances to level the score.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players impact
halftimeSections.text: For Benfica, Enzo Barrenechea has been a standout with his early goal and strong presence in midfield, while Vangelis Pavlidis has been clinical in front of goal. Georgiy Sudakov has also contributed significantly with an assist, showcasing creativity in the attacking third. On Qarabağ's side, Leandro Andrade's goal has been pivotal in keeping them in the game, and Kady Borges has been instrumental in creating big chances. However, Camilo Duran's missed opportunities could prove costly if the trend continues into the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw Benfica dominate the early momentum with consistent attacking spells, particularly in the opening minutes where they built pressure leading to their first goal. They maintained control for much of the half, with notable surges around the times of their goals. Qarabağ, however, fought back with a strong shift in momentum towards the latter stages, especially around the time of their goal and in the closing minutes of the half. This late push indicates they could come out with renewed energy after the break.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: The second half is likely to be an open affair as both teams have shown they can exploit defensive lapses. Benfica will aim to capitalize on their home advantage and sustain their attacking rhythm to extend the lead. Qarabağ, trailing by just one goal, will push for an equalizer, likely focusing on quick transitions and capitalizing on set pieces. Tactical adjustments at half-time could play a key role, with Benfica possibly looking to tighten defensively while Qarabağ might introduce fresh legs to maintain their late first-half momentum.
